text:
It was on a rainy afternoon when I saw her sitting alone by the window at the café. She had long, dark hair that framed an unusually expressive face. I approached her.

"Looking at your book?" I asked.

"What about it?" She looked up, surprised.

"It's about lost souls. Haven't finished it yet, but I enjoy imagining the characters."

She smiled, a warm touch to her lips. "Do you think the main character finds his way?"

I pondered for a moment. "Probably. It's hopeful. Maybe someday they do."

We talked for hours about everything and nothing. We laughed. We shared sorrowful tales of lost love. My own story felt like a distant echo. She was kind.

Eventually she bid me farewell, giving me a card. "Be kind, always."

I carried that card everywhere, until one rainy evening. I sat alone on a park bench, the rain tapping impatiently upon the metal. I absentmindedly brushed against
